text: Should the M0 plan require U09 to realize the CI substrate with a live GitHub Actions run for the exact revision, or stop at local workflow simulation?
why_it_matters: The Charter makes GitHub Actions results blocking, while local AST and synthetic-job tests are only a substitute for provider triggers, permissions, cancellation, artifact retention, and required-check behavior. A live run may require later push/branch-protection authority that the local factory does not implicitly have.
unlocks: The U07 isolation link, github-actions substrate record, U09 verification command/evidence, and whether the M0 exit gate can close without a CI waiver.

options.label: Require live realization
options.description: Register local simulation as the substitute and make U09 pass against a live GitHub Actions run bound to the exact revision; pros: satisfies SM-04 and validates real provider behavior, cons: needs repository push/trigger and branch-protection coordination when U09 executes.

options.label: Park live realization
options.description: Complete local workflow/aggregator tests and leave the GitHub Actions substrate outstanding for a later authorized run; pros: no external mutation or credential dependency during implementation, cons: the factory and M0 gate remain incomplete until a human explicitly approves the substitute or realizes it.

options.label: Change the M0 gate
options.description: Revise the Charter/domain specs so static/local validation is sufficient; pros: fully local delivery, cons: weakens already verified blocking success metrics and requires specification rework before planning can resume.

default: live_realization — plan a bounded, separately authorized live GitHub Actions realization in U09; do not treat local simulation as final CI evidence.

answer.text: Option 1: require U09 to realize the CI substrate with a live GitHub Actions run for the exact revision. Local workflow simulation is preliminary evidence only; the plan must separately request authorization before any push or branch-protection change and retain the live run, artifact, permission, trigger, cancellation, and required-check evidence.
